source: Tier-1 deep audit — 3 Claude general-purpose sub-agents on runtime clusters, every claim Tier-1 verified (reproduced or code-definitive). Realist/unbiased: only reproduced/code-definitive = CONFIRMED; unverified-but-credible = SUSPECTED.

findings.name: resources/list (ListResourcesRequestSchema)
findings.symptom: getTree() returns {tree: FileItem[]} (array of objects) but handler does String(tree.tree||'').split('\n') -> 'String([{..},{..}])' = '[object Object],[object Object]'. Every MCP resources/list returns ONE bogus resource uri 'file://[object Object],[object Object]' instead of the workspace file list.
findings.fix: Iterate the FileItem[] tree, flatten non-dir nodes to their relativePath, build file:// uris from those.
findings.evidence: Live GET /api/workspace/tree returns tree as an array of objects [{name,relativePath,isDirectory,...}] -> String(array) = [object Object],...; code mcp/server.ts:169.

findings.name: grep_search (+ server/terminal.ts:124)
findings.symptom: grep_search builds shell string `grep -rnI "${query}" .` and passes it to TerminalManager.execute which split(/\s+/) tokenizes and runs execFile (no shell). The literal double-quotes become part of argv tokens. Multi-word query 'export function' -> argv ['grep','-rnI','"export','function"','.'] -> grep: function": No such file or directory. Single-word 'providers' -> pattern arg '"providers"' (with literal quotes) -> 0 matches. grep_search returns wrong/empty results for EVERY query (functional regression of the exec->execFile security fix).
findings.fix: Pass the query as one argv element without surrounding quotes (a structured grep helper taking argv), not interpolated into a whitespace-tokenized string.
findings.evidence: Reproduced deterministically: execFile('grep',['-rnI','"export','function"','.']) -> stderr 'grep: function": No such file or directory'; single-word pattern '"providers"' -> 0 stdout lines.

findings.name: ReAct multi-step assistant history omits tool_calls (+ providers.ts:684 openrouter, :834 anthropic verbatim pass-through)
findings.symptom: server.ts:727 pushes {role:'assistant', content: result.text} with NO tool_calls field; then :804 pushes {role:'tool', name, tool_call_id, content}. openrouter (providers.ts:684 messages: config.messages) and anthropic (:834 messages: nonSystemMessages) send this history VERBATIM. Strict OpenAI-compatible models + Anthropic reject a role:'tool' message not preceded by an assistant message carrying matching tool_calls/tool_use -> step 2+ of a tool-using ReAct returns HTTP 400. Multi-step tool ReAct is broken on anthropic + strict openrouter/openai models. SAFE on gemini (providers.ts:602 maps every non-system msg incl role:'tool' -> 'user') and ollama-local incl 480b-cloud (lenient) — the primary path, which is why it was never caught.
findings.fix: On the assistant turn push tool_calls alongside content; and in each cloud mapper translate role:'tool'+tool_call_id into that provider's tool-result shape (OpenAI tool message after assistant.tool_calls; Anthropic user message with tool_result content block; Gemini already OK).
findings.evidence: Code: server.ts:727 (no tool_calls) + :804 (role tool); providers.ts:684 & :834 verbatim. Live repro on anthropic blocked by concurrent fleet load (server saturated) — to finalize after fleet completes; code path is unambiguous.

findings.name: claimDeliveries (sqlite) — no requeue of stranded claims
findings.symptom: sqlite path tags due rows status='claimed_<tok>' then selects them; nothing ANYWHERE ever resets status LIKE 'claimed%' back to 'pending'. If the worker crashes/restarts between claim and markDelivery, those webhook deliveries are stranded forever (never resent, never dead-lettered). The pg path (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, line 574) sets status='claimed' and shares the same orphan risk on crash.
findings.fix: Add a reclaim sweep: UPDATE webhook_deliveries SET status='pending' WHERE status LIKE 'claimed%' AND created_at < (now - staleWindow), run before each claim.
findings.evidence: grep across store/index.ts + webhooks/*: only sites that touch 'claimed' are the two claim writes (574,583) + a pending COUNT (64); no requeue/reclaim exists.

findings.name: ollama-local streaming branch drops tool_calls
findings.symptom: The streaming branch (437-469) forwards only parsed.message.content and returns {text,source,modelUsed} — it never reads parsed.message.tool_calls nor runs extractTextToolCalls(fullText), unlike the non-stream branch (483-492). Any caller with stream:true on ollama-local (/api/ai/generate stream, ai.generateTextStream, /api/generate SSE) loses all tool calls -> a streaming ReAct can't call tools. The MAIN agent loop uses stream:false (server.ts:715) so it is unaffected.
findings.fix: In the stream loop, capture parsed.message.tool_calls when present, and run extractTextToolCalls(fullText) before the final return (mirror 483-492).
findings.evidence: Code: 437-469 has no tool_calls reference; 483-492 (non-stream) parses tool_calls + extractTextToolCalls fallback.

findings.name: cacheKey omits workspaceRoot
findings.symptom: cacheKey = `${tenantId}:${tool}:sha256(args)` does NOT include ctx.workspaceRoot. Two requests, same tenant + same args (read_file/list_tree/grep_search/...) but different workspaceRoot collide -> stale content from workspace A served for workspace B. Only active when MCP_CACHE_TTL_MS>0 (default 0 = cache disabled), so latent.
findings.fix: Fold ctx.workspaceRoot into the cache key.
findings.evidence: Code: tool-interceptors.ts:124-126 hashes only args; cacheTtl() default 0 (line 123).

findings.name: modelUsed undefined after fallback
findings.symptom: providers.ts:189 `if (prov !== config.provider) resolvedConfig.model = undefined` (intentional, so each fallback provider uses its own default). But executeProvider(resolvedConfig) then returns `modelUsed: config.model` = undefined -> any response that fell back reports model: undefined (traceability/billing attribution lost exactly when the chain switched providers).
findings.fix: In each adapter return `modelUsed: config.model || <that provider's default>` (the same default used to build the request).
findings.evidence: Code: providers.ts:184-189 sets resolvedConfig.model=undefined on provider switch; adapters return modelUsed: config.model.

findings.name: processDeliveries no per-row try/catch
findings.symptom: for (const r of rows) await deliverOne(r) (outbound.ts:62) — deliverOne not wrapped; the worker wrapper's .catch (line 71) catches the WHOLE batch, so one deliverOne throw aborts all remaining rows in that batch, leaving them claimed-and-stuck (compounds D-004).
findings.fix: await deliverOne(r).catch(logAndContinue) per row inside the loop.
findings.evidence: Code: outbound.ts:62 bare await in loop; :71 setInterval(()=>processDeliveries().catch(()=>{})) catches batch-level only.

findings.name: custom-openai env key unresolved + db.data.keys unguarded
findings.symptom: getEnvKeyName('custom-openai') returns '' so an env-configured custom endpoint never joins keyPool; line ~750 indexes db.data.keys['custom-openai-endpoint'] without the ||{} guard hasKey uses -> TypeError if db.data.keys undefined.
findings.fix: Add custom-openai case to getEnvKeyName; guard db.data.keys.

findings.name: git-status overlay misclassifies staged/deleted/renamed
findings.symptom: flag=line.substring(0,2).trim() collapses the porcelain XY columns: staged-add 'A ' -> 'A' -> reported 'untracked'; deleted ('D '/' D') and renamed ('R ') -> 'D'/'R' match NO case -> status 'none' -> DROPPED (deleted/renamed files show no git badge in the workspace tree); the `flag==='Staged'` branch is dead code (git porcelain never emits 'Staged').
findings.fix: Parse X (staged, col0) and Y (worktree, col1) separately and map D->deleted, R->renamed, A(col0)->staged.
findings.evidence: Code files.ts:114-122: cases only handle ??/A/M/MM/Staged; D and R fall through to 'none'.

findings.name: verifyWebhook split('=') truncates values containing '='
findings.symptom: header parsed via kv.split('=') taking [0]/[1]; any '=' in the value (base64 padding/future fields) truncates the signature -> false negatives.
findings.fix: split('=') then slice(1).join('=') or a regex.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; no current input triggers it.

findings.name: evictIfNeeded only runs on new key
findings.symptom: evictIfNeeded gated behind !buckets.has(tenantId); an existing idle tenant set never triggers eviction, so churn of returning tenants can exceed MAX_BUCKETS.
findings.fix: Call evictIfNeeded unconditionally (cheap when under cap).

findings.name: subscription handler catch conflates unknown-plan vs DB error
findings.symptom: catch{} swallows both unknown-plan and transient DB errors -> a valid plan with a DB error is reported handled:false-ish; Stripe won't retry -> tenant stuck on old plan.
findings.fix: Distinguish unknown-plan (swallow) from DB error (rethrow -> 500 -> Stripe retries).

findings.name: streaming tokens lost when done chunk lacks eval_duration
findings.symptom: Return with tokensPerSec only fires when parsed.done && eval_count && eval_duration all truthy; a done chunk with eval_count but no eval_duration falls to line 469 returning no tokens -> billing meter under-counts.
findings.fix: Capture eval_count/duration from the done chunk regardless; compute tps when duration>0.

findings.name: firstUserMsg.content.slice/.length assumes string
findings.symptom: server.ts:866 firstUserMsg.content.slice(0,40) + .content.length with NO guard. A user message whose content is absent/non-string (multimodal or tool-shaped history replay) throws inside the session-persist block -> the just-completed ReAct session save is lost. Edge trigger (normal user content is a string).
findings.fix: const c = typeof firstUserMsg.content === 'string' ? firstUserMsg.content : ''; use c.slice/c.length.
